# Memory System

A lightweight markdown-based persistent memory system for AI coding agents. Zero dependencies.

## Problem
AI coding agents forget everything between sessions. You repeat preferences, re-explain architecture decisions, and the agent makes the same mistakes across sessions and projects.

## Solution
A global markdown memory bank with a lightweight index. The agent reads `~/.opencode/memory/_index.md` at boot (~200 tokens) to get a map of all memories, then loads specific files on demand via grep and file reads.

## Architecture
- `~/.opencode/memory/_index.md` — The map: topics, recent entries, project list
- `decisions/` — Architecture/technical decisions (global)
- `lessons/` — Bugs, patterns, gotchas (global)
- `preferences/` — Coding style, workflow rules (global)
- `handoffs/` — Session handoffs
- `projects/<name>/` — Per-project: overview.md, decisions/, lessons/

## How to install
```bash
git clone https://github.com/cvmgxd/memory-system.git
mkdir -p ~/.opencode/memory/{decisions,lessons,preferences,handoffs,projects,templates}
cp memory-system/templates/*.md ~/.opencode/memory/templates/
cp memory-system/commands/*.md .opencode/commands/
# Then add the Memory Protocol block to ~/.config/opencode/AGENTS.md
```

## Key design choices
- Boot protocol reads only _index.md (~200 tokens)
- Agent auto-writes when it detects durable decisions/lessons/preferences
- grep for search, no search engine
- Global index with per-project isolation
- Zero infrastructure: no databases, servers, API keys, npm packages
- Works with: OpenCode, Claude Code, Cursor, any agent that reads files

## Commands
- /memory save — save durable context from session
- /memory index — rebuild index from all entries
- /memory handoff — write end-of-session summary

## Tags
ai-agent-memory, persistent-memory, opencode, coding-agent, markdown-memory, agent-context, memory-bank, cross-session-memory, zero-dependency, local-first
